endpoints.name: Modify an environment by ID. If you try to patch the environment by setting both required and requiredApprovalTags, it will result in an error. Users can specify either required approvals for all flags in an environment or those with specific tags, but not both. Only customers on an Enterprise plan can require approval for flag updates with either mechanism.
endpoints.description: Modify an environment by ID. If you try to patch the environment by setting both required and requiredApprovalTags, it will result in an error. Users can specify either required approvals for all flags in an environment or those with specific tags, but not both. Only customers on an Enterprise plan can require approval for flag updates with either mechanism.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LAUNCHDARKLY_API_KEY}} with your LAUNCHDARKLY API KEY. Replace {{PROJECTKEY}} with a real projectkey. Replace {{ENVIRONMENTKEY}} with a real environmentkey.

endpoints.notes: Replace {{LAUNCHDARKLY_API_KEY}} with your LAUNCHDARKLY API KEY. Replace {{SOURCE}} with a real source.

endpoints.name: Search users in LaunchDarkly based on their last active date, or a search query. It should not be used to enumerate all users in LaunchDarkly-- use the List users API resource.
endpoints.description: Search users in LaunchDarkly based on their last active date, or a search query. It should not be used to enumerate all users in LaunchDarkly-- use the List users API resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LAUNCHDARKLY_API_KEY}} with your LAUNCHDARKLY API KEY. Replace {{PROJECTKEY}} with a real projectkey. Replace {{ENVIRONMENTKEY}} with a real environmentkey.

endpoints.name: List all users in the environment. Includes the total count of users. In each page, there will be up to 'limit' users returned (default 20). This is useful for exporting all users in the system for further analysis. Paginated collections will include a next link containing a URL with the next set of elements in the collection.
endpoints.description: List all users in the environment. Includes the total count of users. In each page, there will be up to 'limit' users returned (default 20).
